articles.headline: India Unveils 'Vision 2047: Sustainable Urban Mobility Mission' to Transform City Transport
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the ambitious 'Vision 2047: Sustainable Urban Mobility Mission', aiming for a paradigm shift in urban transportation across India.
• The mission focuses on integrating public transport, promoting non-motorized transport, and adopting green technologies like electric vehicles and hydrogen fuel cells.
• It includes significant investment in metro rail expansion, high-capacity bus systems, and digital ticketing solutions.
• Emphasis will be placed on last-mile connectivity and citizen-centric planning with active participation from states and urban local bodies.
• A dedicated fund and a new regulatory framework are proposed to ensure timely implementation and coordination.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Mission: Vision 2047: Sustainable Urban Mobility Mission.
Key focus areas: Public transport integration, non-motorized transport, green technologies.
Proposed investments: Metro rail, high-capacity bus systems, digital ticketing.
Implementation body: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (likely).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of sustainable urban mobility for India's economic growth and environmental sustainability.
Analyze the challenges in implementing large-scale urban transport projects in India and suggest measures to overcome them.
Evaluate the potential impact of integrating green technologies and non-motorized transport on urban air quality and public health.
Examine the role of cooperative federalism in achieving the goals of the Sustainable Urban Mobility Mission.
articles.imageDescription: A futuristic depiction of an Indian city with integrated public transport, electric buses, and cycling infrastructure.
articles.trivia: Globally, cities account for over 70% of global CO2 emissions and use two-thirds of the world's energy.

articles.headline: India Lifts ICC Champions Trophy 2025 for a Record Third Time in Dubai
articles.explainer:
• India defeated Australia in a thrilling final in Dubai to clinch the 2025 ICC Champions Trophy, marking their third title victory.
• This historic win solidifies India's dominance in One Day International (ODI) cricket and sparked widespread celebrations across the nation.
• The tournament showcased exceptional performances from both established stars and emerging talents in the Indian squad.
• The victory is expected to further boost the popularity of cricket and inspire young athletes in India.
• The economic impact of such a major sporting achievement includes increased sports tourism, merchandising, and advertising revenue.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of Indian Society
Role of sports in national integration and soft power
prelimsPoints: Event: 2025 ICC Champions Trophy.
Winner: India (third title).
Venue of Final: Dubai.
Opponent in Final: Australia.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of sports in fostering national identity and unity in a diverse country like India.
Analyze how major sporting achievements contribute to a nation's soft power and international image.
Examine the economic ecosystem surrounding major sporting events in India and its contribution to GDP.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for promoting sports culture and infrastructure at the grassroots level in India.
articles.imageDescription: Indian cricket team captain holding the ICC Champions Trophy aloft with teammates celebrating.
articles.trivia: The ICC Champions Trophy is considered the second most prestigious tournament in ODI cricket after the Cricket World Cup.

articles.headline: Renowned Carnatic Musician Garimella Balakrishna Prasad Passes Away at 76
articles.explainer:
• Noted Carnatic vocalist and musicologist, Dr. Garimella Balakrishna Prasad, passed away at the age of 76.
• Prasad was highly regarded for his profound knowledge of classical music and his soulful renditions of Annamacharya compositions.
• He served as the first principal of the Annamacharya Project of Tirumala Tirupati Devasthanams (TTD), dedicating his life to preserving and propagating the saint-composer's works.
• His contributions earned him numerous accolades, including the prestigious Sangeet Natak Akademi Award.
• His demise marks a significant loss to the Carnatic music fraternity and the preservation of Telugu classical heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Personalities in Indian Art and Culture
prelimsPoints: Personality: Garimella Balakrishna Prasad.
Field: Carnatic vocalist and musicologist.
Known for: Renditions of Annamacharya compositions.
Awards: Sangeet Natak Akademi Award.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of saint-composers like Annamacharya in the evolution of South Indian classical music.
Analyze the role of institutions and individuals in preserving and promoting India's diverse classical art forms.
Examine the challenges faced by traditional art forms in the contemporary era and suggest measures for their sustenance.
articles.imageDescription: A black and white photograph of Garimella Balakrishna Prasad performing Carnatic music.
articles.trivia: Annamacharya, also known as Annamayya, is considered the 'Pada-kavita Pitamaha' (grand-father of song-poetry) of Telugu literature.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Reinforces Commitment to Free and Open Indo-Pacific
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming both nations' commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• Discussions focused on enhancing defense cooperation, maritime security, and economic partnerships, particularly in infrastructure development and supply chain resilience.
• Both sides emphasized the importance of multilateral cooperation and adherence to international law in addressing regional challenges.
• Joint exercises and technology transfer in critical and emerging technologies were identified as key areas for future collaboration.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence of strategic interests between India and Japan amidst evolving geopolitical dynamics.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: Event: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue.
Key focus: Free and Open Indo-Pacific, defense, maritime security, economic partnership.
Areas of collaboration: Joint exercises, technology transfer.
Context: Evolving geopolitical dynamics in the Indo-Pacific region.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-Japan Special Strategic and Global Partnership in maintaining regional stability in the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss how India's Act East Policy aligns with Japan's vision for a free and open Indo-Pacific.
Evaluate the role of bilateral defense and security cooperation between India and Japan in countering maritime challenges and ensuring freedom of navigation.
Examine the potential for India-Japan collaboration in critical technologies and infrastructure development in third countries.
articles.imageDescription: Foreign Ministers of India and Japan shaking hands during the strategic dialogue.
articles.trivia: Japan is a significant investor in India's infrastructure, including the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail project (Bullet Train).

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Focuses on Taming Inflation and Sustaining Growth
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to maintain the repo rate at its current level for the sixth consecutive meeting.
• The decision comes amidst persistent global inflationary pressures and a focus on ensuring durable disinflation while supporting economic growth.
• RBI Governor highlighted the need for vigilance against rising food and energy prices, which could impact headline inflation.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to calibrating monetary policy to achieve the medium-term inflation target of 4% within a band of +/- 2%.
• Experts suggest that while growth remains robust, global uncertainties and domestic supply-side issues warrant a cautious approach.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: Body: Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC).
Decision: Repo rate maintained.
Objective: Disinflation, economic growth.
Inflation target: 4% (+/- 2%).
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global economic scenario.
Discuss the various tools available to the RBI under its monetary policy framework and their effectiveness.
Evaluate the impact of global supply chain disruptions and geopolitical events on India's inflation trajectory and RBI's policy decisions.
Examine the role of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in ensuring transparency and accountability in monetary policy formulation.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building facade in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members – three officials of the RBI and three external experts nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test of Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful completion of a crucial test for its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program.
• The test involved a precise landing experiment of the RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) at a designated airstrip, simulating re-entry conditions.
• This achievement marks a significant step towards developing a cost-effective and sustainable space transportation system for India.
• Reusable launch vehicles are vital for reducing the cost of space access, enabling more frequent missions, and facilitating future space exploration initiatives.
• ISRO aims to develop a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) fully reusable launch vehicle in the coming decades.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Space Technology
prelimsPoints: Organization: ISRO.
Achievement: Successful test of Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV).
Specific test: Landing experiment of RLV-TD.
Goal: Cost-effective, sustainable space transportation system.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program and its global standing.
Analyze the economic benefits and environmental advantages of developing reusable space launch systems.
Examine the challenges and technological hurdles ISRO faces in developing a fully reusable launch vehicle.
Evaluate India's achievements in space technology and its potential to contribute to global space exploration efforts.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) descending for landing.
articles.trivia: The RLV-TD is designed to act as a flying testbed to evaluate various technologies, including hypersonic flight, autonomous landing, and powered cruise flight.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Right to Digital Privacy, Sets Guidelines for Data Protection
articles.explainer:
• In a landmark judgment, the Supreme Court of India reaffirmed the fundamental 'Right to Digital Privacy' as an intrinsic part of Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty).
• The ruling came in a case challenging the indiscriminate collection and use of personal data by private entities and government agencies.
• The Court laid down stringent guidelines for data collection, processing, and storage, emphasizing consent, purpose limitation, and data minimization.
• It directed the government to expedite the enactment of a comprehensive data protection law, consistent with the principles outlined in the judgment.
• This decision is expected to have far-reaching implications for technology companies, data governance, and individual rights in the digital age.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary Ministries and Departments of the Government; pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity.
prelimsPoints: Court: Supreme Court of India.
Verdict: Right to Digital Privacy upheld as part of Article 21.
Key principles: Consent, purpose limitation, data minimization.
Directive: Expedite comprehensive data protection law.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's judgment on the Right to Digital Privacy for individual liberties and state surveillance in India.
Discuss the challenges in balancing national security concerns with the fundamental right to privacy in the digital realm.
Evaluate the adequacy of existing legal frameworks in India to protect personal data and suggest necessary reforms.
Examine the global best practices in data protection and how India can align its policies to meet international standards.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on law books with a digital padlock icon superimposed, symbolizing digital justice.
articles.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) first declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

articles.headline: Ministry of Environment Launches 'Coastal Ecosystem Restoration Project' for Biodiversity Conservation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) launched a multi-state 'Coastal Ecosystem Restoration Project'.
• The initiative aims to restore degraded coastal habitats, including mangroves, coral reefs, and salt marshes, along India's vast coastline.
• It integrates local communities in conservation efforts, providing alternative livelihoods and raising awareness about coastal biodiversity.
• The project utilizes advanced remote sensing and GIS technologies for monitoring and impact assessment.
• This initiative is critical for enhancing coastal resilience against climate change impacts like sea-level rise and extreme weather events.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: Project: Coastal Ecosystem Restoration Project.
Launched by: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC).
Focus: Mangroves, coral reefs, salt marshes.
Objective: Restore degraded coastal habitats, enhance climate resilience.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological and economic significance of healthy coastal ecosystems for India.
Analyze the major threats to India's coastal biodiversity and the challenges in their conservation.
Evaluate the effectiveness of community-based conservation models in coastal regions.
Examine how coastal ecosystem restoration contributes to India's climate change adaptation and mitigation strategies.
articles.imageDescription: A vibrant image of a mangrove forest with diverse marine life, symbolizing coastal biodiversity.
articles.trivia: Mangroves are often called 'coastal guardians' as they protect shorelines from erosion and storm surges, and serve as nurseries for many marine species.

articles.headline: India-Nepal Joint Working Group Concludes Talks on Border Management and Security
articles.explainer:
• The 18th meeting of the India-Nepal Joint Working Group (JWG) on Border Management and Security concluded in Kathmandu.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to maintaining peace and tranquillity along the open border and addressing cross-border crimes.
• Discussions included enhancing cooperation on intelligence sharing, curbing human trafficking, drug smuggling, and illegal arms trade.
• The JWG reviewed progress on border infrastructure development and agreed on measures to streamline movement of people and goods.
• This dialogue is crucial for strengthening bilateral ties and ensuring mutual security along the 1,800-km long shared border.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: Event: India-Nepal Joint Working Group (JWG) meeting.
Focus: Border management, security, cross-border crime.
Location: Kathmandu.
Significance: Maintaining peace along the open border.
mainsPoints: Discuss the unique nature of the India-Nepal open border and the challenges it poses for border management and security.
Analyze the various forms of cross-border crimes prevalent along the India-Nepal border and their implications for internal security.
Evaluate the effectiveness of bilateral mechanisms like the Joint Working Group in addressing security concerns and fostering cooperation.
Examine the role of local communities in border management and the importance of socio-economic development in border areas.
articles.imageDescription: A map highlighting the India-Nepal border, with symbols representing cooperation and security.
articles.trivia: The India-Nepal border is unique for being an open border, allowing free movement of people, a provision that dates back to the 1950 Treaty of Peace and Friendship.

articles.headline: National Health Mission Expands Telemedicine Services to Remote Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare announced a significant expansion of telemedicine services under the National Health Mission (NHM).
• The initiative aims to enhance healthcare accessibility in remote, rural, and underserved regions of the country.
• The expansion includes upgrading digital infrastructure, training healthcare workers, and integrating AI-powered diagnostic tools.
• Telemedicine services will cover a wider range of specialties, including mental health, chronic disease management, and specialist consultations.
• This move is expected to bridge the gap in healthcare delivery, reduce patient travel, and improve health outcomes, particularly for vulnerable populations.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Scheme: National Health Mission (NHM).
Initiative: Expansion of telemedicine services.
Target areas: Remote, rural, underserved regions.
Key features: Digital infrastructure, training, AI diagnostics, wider specialties.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of telemedicine in transforming healthcare delivery in a vast and diverse country like India.
Analyze the challenges in implementing and scaling up telemedicine services, especially in rural and remote areas.
Evaluate the role of digital infrastructure and human resource development in ensuring equitable access to telemedicine.
Examine how telemedicine can contribute to achieving the goals of universal health coverage and the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) related to health.
articles.imageDescription: A doctor interacting with a patient via a video call on a tablet, symbolizing telemedicine.
articles.trivia: eSanjeevani, the national telemedicine service, has served millions of patients, becoming the world's largest government-owned telemedicine platform.

articles.headline: India's Services PMI Reaches Multi-Year High, Signalling Robust Economic Momentum
articles.explainer:
• India's Services Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) surged to a multi-year high in February, indicating strong growth momentum in the services sector.
• The expansion was driven by robust demand, new business gains, and increased business confidence, particularly in financial services and IT.
• This positive trend complements strong manufacturing data, suggesting a broad-based economic recovery and resilience.
• The rise in services PMI is expected to contribute positively to job creation and overall GDP growth in the coming quarters.
• However, input cost inflation remains a concern, which could potentially impact profit margins and consumer prices.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Growth and Development
prelimsPoints: Indicator: Services Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI).
Trend: Multi-year high in February.
Drivers: Strong demand, new business, business confidence.
Concern: Input cost inflation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the Services PMI as a leading economic indicator for India's economic health.
Discuss the factors contributing to the robust growth in India's services sector and its role in overall economic development.
Examine the challenges posed by input cost inflation for businesses in the services sector and its potential impact on consumer prices.
Evaluate the government's policies aimed at boosting the services sector and creating employment opportunities.
articles.imageDescription: A chart showing an upward trend in India's Services PMI, indicating economic growth.
articles.trivia: A PMI reading above 50 indicates expansion in the sector, while a reading below 50 indicates contraction.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Global South Interests at G20 Sherpa Meeting
articles.explainer:
• India's G20 Sherpa actively championed the concerns and priorities of the Global South at the recent G20 Sherpa meeting.
• Key issues highlighted included equitable access to finance for sustainable development, climate justice, and food and energy security.
• India emphasized the need for reforms in multilateral development banks to better serve the needs of developing countries.
• The Sherpa meeting aimed to finalize the agenda and draft declarations for the upcoming G20 Leaders' Summit.
• India continues to play a pivotal role in bridging the gap between developed and developing nations within the G20 framework.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: Forum: G20 Sherpa meeting.
India's role: Advocating for Global South interests.
Key issues: Finance access, climate justice, food/energy security.
Focus: Reforms in multilateral development banks.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's growing leadership role in representing the interests of the Global South within international forums like the G20.
Analyze the challenges faced by developing countries in achieving sustainable development and climate justice in the current global economic order.
Evaluate the need for reforms in multilateral development banks to enhance their responsiveness to the needs of the Global South.
Examine how India's foreign policy prioritizes multilateralism and cooperation to address global challenges.
articles.imageDescription: A collage of flags from G20 member countries with a focus on India's flag, symbolizing international diplomacy.
articles.trivia: The G20 comprises 19 countries and the European Union, representing around 85% of global GDP, over 75% of global trade, and about two-thirds of the world population.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Reforms for Judicial Appointments and Accountability
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Law and Justice submitted a report recommending significant reforms in the system of judicial appointments and accountability.
• The report advocated for greater transparency in the Collegium system and suggested a more inclusive selection process involving greater public scrutiny.
• It proposed a clearer mechanism for addressing complaints against judges and enhancing judicial ethics.
• The committee also highlighted the need to fill judicial vacancies expeditiously to reduce pendency of cases.
• These recommendations are aimed at strengthening the independence of the judiciary while ensuring its accountability to the public.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary Ministries and Departments of the Government; pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
prelimsPoints: Body: Parliamentary Standing Committee on Law and Justice.
Recommendations: Reforms in judicial appointments, greater transparency in Collegium system.
Focus: Accountability, judicial ethics, filling vacancies.
Objective: Strengthen judicial independence and accountability.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the existing Collegium system for judicial appointments in India and the arguments for and against its reform.
Discuss the importance of judicial independence and accountability in a democratic setup and the challenges in balancing them.
Analyze the recommendations of the Parliamentary Committee for improving the transparency and efficiency of judicial appointments.
Examine the impact of judicial vacancies and case pendency on the delivery of justice in India and suggest comprehensive solutions.
articles.imageDescription: A depiction of the Indian Parliament building and the Supreme Court building, symbolizing the interaction between legislative and judicial bodies.
articles.trivia: The Collegium system, which evolved through Supreme Court judgments, currently governs the appointment and transfer of judges to the higher judiciary.

articles.headline: New Report Highlights Increasing Frequency and Intensity of Heatwaves in India
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive report released by the India Meteorological Department (IMD) highlights a concerning increase in the frequency and intensity of heatwaves across India.
• The report attributes this trend primarily to climate change, coupled with localized factors like urbanization and deforestation.
• It projects a further rise in heatwave days and their severity, posing significant risks to public health, agriculture, and labor productivity.
• The study calls for enhanced early warning systems, heat action plans, and climate-resilient infrastructure to mitigate the impacts.
• This underscores the urgent need for India to strengthen its climate adaptation strategies and reduce greenhouse gas emissions.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Report by: India Meteorological Department (IMD).
Finding: Increasing frequency and intensity of heatwaves in India.
Cause: Climate change, urbanization, deforestation.
Recommendations: Early warning systems, heat action plans, climate-resilient infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic and environmental impacts of increasing heatwave frequency and intensity in India.
Analyze the role of climate change and anthropogenic factors in exacerbating heatwave conditions across the country.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing heat action plans and early warning systems in mitigating heatwave-related risks.
Suggest comprehensive policy measures and technological interventions required to build climate resilience against extreme heat events in India.
articles.imageDescription: A thermometer showing high temperatures against a backdrop of a parched landscape, symbolizing extreme heat.
articles.trivia: A heatwave is declared when the maximum temperature of a station reaches at least 40°C in plains and at least 30°C in hilly regions, and the departure from normal is 4.5°C to 6.4°C or more.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Achieve Breakthrough in Indigenous Battery Technology for Electric Vehicles
articles.explainer:
• Researchers at a leading Indian institute have announced a significant breakthrough in developing advanced indigenous battery technology for electric vehicles (EVs).
• The new battery chemistry promises higher energy density, faster charging capabilities, and improved safety compared to conventional lithium-ion batteries.
• This innovation is crucial for reducing India's reliance on imported battery components and boosting domestic EV manufacturing.
• The technology is currently undergoing scalability tests for commercial production and integration into various EV models.
• This development aligns with India's National Mission on Transformative Mobility and Battery Storage and its broader 'Make in India' initiative.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Achievement: Breakthrough in indigenous battery technology for EVs.
Benefits: Higher energy density, faster charging, improved safety.
Significance: Reduces import reliance, boosts domestic EV manufacturing.
Policy alignment: National Mission on Transformative Mobility, Make in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of advanced battery technology in accelerating India's transition to electric vehicles and achieving energy security.
Analyze the challenges India faces in developing a robust domestic battery manufacturing ecosystem and suggest policy interventions.
Evaluate the environmental and economic benefits of indigenizing battery technology for EVs.
Examine how this breakthrough contributes to India's broader goals of sustainable mobility and reducing carbon emissions.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image of a new battery cell with an electric car in the background, symbolizing innovation in EV technology.
articles.trivia: India aims to have 30% of its private cars, 70% of commercial vehicles, and 80% of two and three-wheelers electric by 2030.

articles.headline: Government Launches Nationwide Awareness Campaign on Mental Health and Well-being
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, in collaboration with various NGOs, launched a nationwide awareness campaign on mental health and well-being.
• The campaign aims to destigmatize mental illness, promote early intervention, and educate the public about available support systems.
• It targets diverse demographics, including youth, working professionals, and rural communities, through multi-platform outreach.
• Special focus is given to integrating mental health services at the primary healthcare level and training frontline workers.
• This initiative underscores the government's commitment to addressing the growing burden of mental health issues and promoting holistic well-being.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: Campaign: Nationwide awareness campaign on mental health.
Launched by: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (with NGOs).
Objectives: Destigmatize mental illness, promote early intervention, educate public.
Focus: Primary healthcare integration, frontline worker training.
mainsPoints: Discuss the increasing prevalence of mental health issues in India and the socio-economic factors contributing to this trend.
Analyze the challenges in addressing mental health concerns in India, particularly the issues of stigma, lack of awareness, and inadequate infrastructure.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives and policies in promoting mental health and well-being across different sections of society.
Suggest comprehensive strategies for integrating mental health services into primary healthcare and building a supportive ecosystem for individuals with mental illness.
articles.imageDescription: A diverse group of people engaging in positive activities, with a thought bubble symbolizing mental well-being.
articles.trivia: According to the World Health Organization, India accounts for nearly 15% of the global mental health burden.

articles.headline: Government Announces New Credit Guarantee Scheme to Boost MSME Sector
articles.explainer:
• The Union Finance Ministry launched a new Credit Guarantee Scheme specifically designed to provide a significant boost to the Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSME) sector.
• The scheme aims to enhance access to collateral-free institutional credit for MSMEs, reducing their dependency on informal lending sources.
• It offers higher guarantee coverage and simplified application processes, encouraging banks and financial institutions to lend more to the sector.
• This initiative is expected to stimulate investment, foster job creation, and accelerate the growth of the MSME ecosystem.
• The MSME sector is a crucial contributor to India's GDP and employment, making this scheme vital for economic recovery and growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
keyTerms: Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE)
prelimsPoints: Scheme: New Credit Guarantee Scheme for MSMEs.
Launched by: Union Finance Ministry.
Objective: Enhance access to collateral-free institutional credit.
Benefits: Higher guarantee coverage, simplified process.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the MSME sector in India's economic landscape, particularly its role in employment generation and exports.
Analyze the major challenges faced by MSMEs in accessing institutional credit and their implications for growth.
Evaluate the potential impact of the new Credit Guarantee Scheme on the financial health and expansion of the MSME sector.
Examine how government policies and financial interventions contribute to fostering an inclusive and resilient MSME ecosystem in India.
articles.imageDescription: Small gears interlocking with larger gears, symbolizing the MSME sector's contribution to the larger economy.
articles.trivia: The MSME sector contributes approximately 30% to India's GDP and employs over 11 crore people.

articles.headline: India and Sri Lanka Hold High-Level Dialogue on Maritime Security Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka convened a high-level dialogue in Colombo to enhance cooperation on maritime security in the Indian Ocean region.
• Discussions focused on joint surveillance, anti-piracy operations, combating illegal fishing, and intelligence sharing.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to ensuring a safe and secure maritime environment, crucial for regional trade and stability.
• The dialogue also addressed challenges posed by transnational crimes and the need for coordinated responses.
• This engagement underscores the strategic importance of bilateral maritime relations for both countries.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
keyTerms: SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region)
prelimsPoints: Event: High-level dialogue on maritime security.
Participants: India and Sri Lanka.
Location: Colombo.
Focus: Joint surveillance, anti-piracy, illegal fishing, intelligence sharing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the Indian Ocean Region for India's security and economic interests.
Analyze the common maritime security challenges faced by India and Sri Lanka and the need for bilateral cooperation.
Evaluate the effectiveness of joint mechanisms in combating transnational crimes and ensuring maritime safety.
Examine how India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy and SAGAR vision contribute to strengthening maritime partnerships in the region.
articles.imageDescription: Naval ships from India and Sri Lanka conducting a joint exercise in the Indian Ocean.
articles.trivia: India and Sri Lanka conduct several joint naval exercises, including 'SLINEX' and 'Dosti' (trilateral with Maldives), to enhance maritime cooperation.

articles.headline: National Mission on Interlinking of Rivers Reviews Progress, Addresses Environmental Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Special Committee for Interlinking of Rivers (SCILR) under the National Water Development Agency (NWDA) held a review meeting.
• The committee assessed the progress of various river interlinking projects, focusing on the Ken-Betwa Link Project and others.
• Discussions included strategies to accelerate project implementation while addressing environmental impact assessments and rehabilitation concerns.
• Emphasis was placed on ensuring ecological sustainability and equitable water distribution across beneficiary regions.
• The National Mission on Interlinking of Rivers aims to transfer surplus water from water-rich basins to water-deficit basins, enhancing water security.
gsSubTopics: Distribution of key natural resources across the world (including South Asia and the Indian subcontinent); factors responsible for the location of primary, secondary, and tertiary sector industries in various parts of the world (including India).

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: Mission: National Mission on Interlinking of Rivers.
Reviewing body: Special Committee for Interlinking of Rivers (SCILR) under NWDA.
Key project reviewed: Ken-Betwa Link Project.
Objectives: Water security, equitable distribution, environmental sustainability.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential benefits and challenges associated with the National Mission on Interlinking of Rivers in India.
Analyze the environmental, social, and economic implications of large-scale river interlinking projects, using the Ken-Betwa Link as an example.
Evaluate the mechanisms for conducting Environmental Impact Assessments (EIAs) for such projects and suggest measures for their improvement.
Examine how inter-state water disputes can be resolved in the context of river interlinking projects and the role of cooperative federalism.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of a river flowing through a landscape, with lines indicating proposed interlinking canals.
articles.trivia: The Ken-Betwa Link Project is the first project under the National Perspective Plan for interlinking of rivers, aiming to transfer water from the Ken river to the Betwa river.

articles.headline: Archaeological Excavations Uncover New Insights into Indus Valley Civilization Trade Networks
articles.explainer:
• Recent archaeological excavations at sites in Gujarat and Rajasthan have yielded new evidence shedding light on the extensive trade networks of the Indus Valley Civilization (IVC).
• Findings include artifacts of foreign origin and unique seal impressions, suggesting robust maritime and terrestrial trade links with Mesopotamia and other regions.
• The discoveries provide deeper insights into the economic organization, cultural exchanges, and technological advancements of the IVC.
• Researchers are employing advanced dating techniques and material analysis to establish precise timelines and trade routes.
• These ongoing excavations are crucial for a more comprehensive understanding of one of the world's earliest major civilizations.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Ancient Indian History
prelimsPoints: Civilization: Indus Valley Civilization (IVC).
Discovery: New evidence of trade networks.
Sites: Gujarat, Rajasthan.
Evidence: Foreign artifacts, unique seal impressions.
Trade links: Mesopotamia and other regions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the nature and extent of trade networks of the Indus Valley Civilization, both internal and external.
Analyze how archaeological findings contribute to our understanding of the economic and cultural life of ancient civilizations.
Evaluate the significance of maritime trade routes for the prosperity and cultural exchange of the IVC.
Examine the challenges faced by archaeologists in deciphering the Indus script and its implications for understanding IVC society.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic reconstruction of an Indus Valley port city like Lothal, showing trading ships and busy docks.
articles.trivia: Lothal, an ancient port city of the Indus Valley Civilization in Gujarat, had one of the world's earliest known dockyards.

highlights: India launches 'Vision 2047: Sustainable Urban Mobility Mission' to transform city transport and promote green technologies.
India wins the 2025 ICC Champions Trophy for a record third time in Dubai, defeating Australia in the final.
Renowned Carnatic musician and musicologist Garimella Balakrishna Prasad passes away at 76, leaving a significant void in classical music.
Supreme Court upholds 'Right to Digital Privacy' as a fundamental right, issuing strict guidelines for data protection.
ISRO successfully conducts a critical test of its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV), a step towards cost-effective space access.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'Vision 2047: Sustainable Urban Mobility Mission':
1. It aims to integrate public transport and promote non-motorized transport.
2. It explicitly excludes the adoption of electric vehicles and hydrogen fuel cells to focus on traditional public transport.
3. The mission proposes a new regulatory framework and a dedicated fund for implementation.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as the mission focuses on integrating public transport and promoting non-motorized transport. Statement 2 is incorrect; the mission explicitly includes the adoption of green technologies like electric vehicles and hydrogen fuel cells. Statement 3 is correct as a dedicated fund and a new regulatory framework are proposed for timely implementation.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent Supreme Court judgment on 'Right to Digital Privacy', consider the following statements:
1. The Court reaffirmed it as an intrinsic part of Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.
2. It directed the government to repeal the existing data protection law.
3. The judgment emphasized principles like consent, purpose limitation, and data minimization.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as the Supreme Court reaffirmed the Right to Digital Privacy as part of Article 21. Statement 2 is incorrect; the Court directed the government to expedite the enactment of a comprehensive data protection law, not repeal an existing one. Statement 3 is correct as stringent guidelines emphasizing consent, purpose limitation, and data minimization were laid down.

prelimsMocks.question: Garimella Balakrishna Prasad, who recently passed away, was a renowned personality associated with which of the following fields?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Garimella Balakrishna Prasad was a noted Carnatic vocalist and musicologist, known for his renditions of Annamacharya compositions.

prelimsMocks.question: The India-Japan Strategic Dialogue, recently held in New Delhi, primarily focused on which of the following regions?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The dialogue reaffirmed both nations' commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, focusing on defense cooperation and maritime security in the region.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of the new indigenous battery technology for Electric Vehicles (EVs) recently developed by Indian scientists?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The new indigenous battery technology aims to reduce India's reliance on imported battery components, not increase it. Higher energy density, faster charging, and improved safety are stated benefits.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the potential of India's 'Vision 2047: Sustainable Urban Mobility Mission' to address the multifaceted challenges of urbanization and climate change. What are the key hurdles in its implementation, and how can they be overcome?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation. GS3: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.; Environmental pollution and degradation.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the mission and its objectives. Discuss its potential benefits for urban development, environmental sustainability, and quality of life. Identify key implementation hurdles such as funding, inter-agency coordination, land acquisition, public participation, and technological integration. Suggest measures like innovative financing, robust governance, public-private partnerships, and community engagement to overcome these challenges. Conclude with the importance of a holistic approach.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's reaffirmation of the 'Right to Digital Privacy' marks a significant step towards safeguarding individual liberties in the digital age. Discuss the implications of this judgment for data governance, state surveillance, and the responsibilities of technology companies in India. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary. GS3: Cyber security.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by stating the significance of the judgment as an extension of Article 21. Discuss its implications for data governance by emphasizing consent, data minimization, and purpose limitation. Analyze how it sets limits on state surveillance and necessitates a robust data protection law. Examine the increased responsibilities of technology companies regarding user data. Conclude by highlighting the balance between individual rights and public interest in the digital realm.

mainsMocks.question: Despite significant progress in space technology, India faces challenges in achieving fully reusable launch vehicle capabilities. Examine the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's space aspirations and the technological hurdles ISRO must overcome to realize this goal. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology; Space Technology.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce ISRO's RLV program and its long-term vision. Explain the strategic importance, including cost reduction, increased launch frequency, and enhanced access to space for commercial and scientific missions. Detail the technological hurdles such as advanced materials, autonomous landing systems, robust thermal protection, and propulsion systems for re-entry and reuse. Conclude by emphasizing the indigenization aspect and its role in India's self-reliance in space.

quickBytes.text: The 'Vision 2047: Sustainable Urban Mobility Mission' aims to integrate public transport, non-motorized transport, and green technologies.
India won the 2025 ICC Champions Trophy for the third time, defeating Australia in Dubai.
Garimella Balakrishna Prasad, a renowned Carnatic vocalist, was known for his renditions of Annamacharya compositions.
The India-Japan Strategic Dialogue focused on a free and open Indo-Pacific and enhanced defense cooperation.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee maintained the repo rate, prioritizing disinflation while supporting economic growth.
ISRO successfully tested its Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV), crucial for cost-effective space access.
The Supreme Court upheld the 'Right to Digital Privacy' as fundamental under Article 21, emphasizing data protection guidelines.
The Ministry of Environment launched a 'Coastal Ecosystem Restoration Project' for mangroves and coral reefs.
India's Services PMI reached a multi-year high, indicating strong growth momentum in the services sector.
The Indus Valley Civilization's trade networks extended to Mesopotamia, evidenced by recent archaeological findings.

GS1.headline: India Lifts ICC Champions Trophy 2025 for a Record Third Time in Dubai
GS1.explainer:
• India defeated Australia in a thrilling final in Dubai to clinch the 2025 ICC Champions Trophy, marking their third title victory.
• This historic win solidifies India's dominance in One Day International (ODI) cricket and sparked widespread celebrations across the nation.
• The tournament showcased exceptional performances from both established stars and emerging talents in the Indian squad.
• The victory is expected to further boost the popularity of cricket and inspire young athletes in India.
• The economic impact of such a major sporting achievement includes increased sports tourism, merchandising, and advertising revenue.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of Indian Society
Role of sports in national integration and soft power
prelimsPoints: Event: 2025 ICC Champions Trophy.
Winner: India (third title).
Venue of Final: Dubai.
Opponent in Final: Australia.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of sports in fostering national identity and unity in a diverse country like India.
Analyze how major sporting achievements contribute to a nation's soft power and international image.
Examine the economic ecosystem surrounding major sporting events in India and its contribution to GDP.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for promoting sports culture and infrastructure at the grassroots level in India.
GS1.imageDescription: Indian cricket team captain holding the ICC Champions Trophy aloft with teammates celebrating.
GS1.trivia: The ICC Champions Trophy is considered the second most prestigious tournament in ODI cricket after the Cricket World Cup.

GS1.headline: Renowned Carnatic Musician Garimella Balakrishna Prasad Passes Away at 76
GS1.explainer:
• Noted Carnatic vocalist and musicologist, Dr. Garimella Balakrishna Prasad, passed away at the age of 76.
• Prasad was highly regarded for his profound knowledge of classical music and his soulful renditions of Annamacharya compositions.
• He served as the first principal of the Annamacharya Project of Tirumala Tirupati Devasthanams (TTD), dedicating his life to preserving and propagating the saint-composer's works.
• His contributions earned him numerous accolades, including the prestigious Sangeet Natak Akademi Award.
• His demise marks a significant loss to the Carnatic music fraternity and the preservation of Telugu classical heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Personalities in Indian Art and Culture
prelimsPoints: Personality: Garimella Balakrishna Prasad.
Field: Carnatic vocalist and musicologist.
Known for: Renditions of Annamacharya compositions.
Awards: Sangeet Natak Akademi Award.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of saint-composers like Annamacharya in the evolution of South Indian classical music.
Analyze the role of institutions and individuals in preserving and promoting India's diverse classical art forms.
Examine the challenges faced by traditional art forms in the contemporary era and suggest measures for their sustenance.
GS1.imageDescription: A black and white photograph of Garimella Balakrishna Prasad performing Carnatic music.
GS1.trivia: Annamacharya, also known as Annamayya, is considered the 'Pada-kavita Pitamaha' (grand-father of song-poetry) of Telugu literature.

GS1.headline: New Report Highlights Increasing Frequency and Intensity of Heatwaves in India
GS1.explainer:
• A comprehensive report released by the India Meteorological Department (IMD) highlights a concerning increase in the frequency and intensity of heatwaves across India.
• The report attributes this trend primarily to climate change, coupled with localized factors like urbanization and deforestation.
• It projects a further rise in heatwave days and their severity, posing significant risks to public health, agriculture, and labor productivity.
• The study calls for enhanced early warning systems, heat action plans, and climate-resilient infrastructure to mitigate the impacts.
• This underscores the urgent need for India to strengthen its climate adaptation strategies and reduce greenhouse gas emissions.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Report by: India Meteorological Department (IMD).
Finding: Increasing frequency and intensity of heatwaves in India.
Cause: Climate change, urbanization, deforestation.
Recommendations: Early warning systems, heat action plans, climate-resilient infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic and environmental impacts of increasing heatwave frequency and intensity in India.
Analyze the role of climate change and anthropogenic factors in exacerbating heatwave conditions across the country.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing heat action plans and early warning systems in mitigating heatwave-related risks.
Suggest comprehensive policy measures and technological interventions required to build climate resilience against extreme heat events in India.
GS1.imageDescription: A thermometer showing high temperatures against a backdrop of a parched landscape, symbolizing extreme heat.
GS1.trivia: A heatwave is declared when the maximum temperature of a station reaches at least 40°C in plains and at least 30°C in hilly regions, and the departure from normal is 4.5°C to 6.4°C or more.

GS1.headline: National Mission on Interlinking of Rivers Reviews Progress, Addresses Environmental Concerns
GS1.explainer:
• The Special Committee for Interlinking of Rivers (SCILR) under the National Water Development Agency (NWDA) held a review meeting.
• The committee assessed the progress of various river interlinking projects, focusing on the Ken-Betwa Link Project and others.
• Discussions included strategies to accelerate project implementation while addressing environmental impact assessments and rehabilitation concerns.
• Emphasis was placed on ensuring ecological sustainability and equitable water distribution across beneficiary regions.
• The National Mission on Interlinking of Rivers aims to transfer surplus water from water-rich basins to water-deficit basins, enhancing water security.
gsSubTopics: Distribution of key natural resources across the world (including South Asia and the Indian subcontinent); factors responsible for the location of primary, secondary, and tertiary sector industries in various parts of the world (including India).

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: Mission: National Mission on Interlinking of Rivers.
Reviewing body: Special Committee for Interlinking of Rivers (SCILR) under NWDA.
Key project reviewed: Ken-Betwa Link Project.
Objectives: Water security, equitable distribution, environmental sustainability.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential benefits and challenges associated with the National Mission on Interlinking of Rivers in India.
Analyze the environmental, social, and economic implications of large-scale river interlinking projects, using the Ken-Betwa Link as an example.
Evaluate the mechanisms for conducting Environmental Impact Assessments (EIAs) for such projects and suggest measures for their improvement.
Examine how inter-state water disputes can be resolved in the context of river interlinking projects and the role of cooperative federalism.
GS1.imageDescription: An aerial view of a river flowing through a landscape, with lines indicating proposed interlinking canals.
GS1.trivia: The Ken-Betwa Link Project is the first project under the National Perspective Plan for interlinking of rivers, aiming to transfer water from the Ken river to the Betwa river.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Excavations Uncover New Insights into Indus Valley Civilization Trade Networks
GS1.explainer:
• Recent archaeological excavations at sites in Gujarat and Rajasthan have yielded new evidence shedding light on the extensive trade networks of the Indus Valley Civilization (IVC).
• Findings include artifacts of foreign origin and unique seal impressions, suggesting robust maritime and terrestrial trade links with Mesopotamia and other regions.
• The discoveries provide deeper insights into the economic organization, cultural exchanges, and technological advancements of the IVC.
• Researchers are employing advanced dating techniques and material analysis to establish precise timelines and trade routes.
• These ongoing excavations are crucial for a more comprehensive understanding of one of the world's earliest major civilizations.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Ancient Indian History
prelimsPoints: Civilization: Indus Valley Civilization (IVC).
Discovery: New evidence of trade networks.
Sites: Gujarat, Rajasthan.
Evidence: Foreign artifacts, unique seal impressions.
Trade links: Mesopotamia and other regions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the nature and extent of trade networks of the Indus Valley Civilization, both internal and external.
Analyze how archaeological findings contribute to our understanding of the economic and cultural life of ancient civilizations.
Evaluate the significance of maritime trade routes for the prosperity and cultural exchange of the IVC.
Examine the challenges faced by archaeologists in deciphering the Indus script and its implications for understanding IVC society.
GS1.imageDescription: An artistic reconstruction of an Indus Valley port city like Lothal, showing trading ships and busy docks.
GS1.trivia: Lothal, an ancient port city of the Indus Valley Civilization in Gujarat, had one of the world's earliest known dockyards.

GS2.headline: India Unveils 'Vision 2047: Sustainable Urban Mobility Mission' to Transform City Transport
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the ambitious 'Vision 2047: Sustainable Urban Mobility Mission', aiming for a paradigm shift in urban transportation across India.
• The mission focuses on integrating public transport, promoting non-motorized transport, and adopting green technologies like electric vehicles and hydrogen fuel cells.
• It includes significant investment in metro rail expansion, high-capacity bus systems, and digital ticketing solutions.
• Emphasis will be placed on last-mile connectivity and citizen-centric planning with active participation from states and urban local bodies.
• A dedicated fund and a new regulatory framework are proposed to ensure timely implementation and coordination.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Mission: Vision 2047: Sustainable Urban Mobility Mission.
Key focus areas: Public transport integration, non-motorized transport, green technologies.
Proposed investments: Metro rail, high-capacity bus systems, digital ticketing.
Implementation body: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (likely).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of sustainable urban mobility for India's economic growth and environmental sustainability.
Analyze the challenges in implementing large-scale urban transport projects in India and suggest measures to overcome them.
Evaluate the potential impact of integrating green technologies and non-motorized transport on urban air quality and public health.
Examine the role of cooperative federalism in achieving the goals of the Sustainable Urban Mobility Mission.
GS2.imageDescription: A futuristic depiction of an Indian city with integrated public transport, electric buses, and cycling infrastructure.
GS2.trivia: Globally, cities account for over 70% of global CO2 emissions and use two-thirds of the world's energy.

GS2.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Reinforces Commitment to Free and Open Indo-Pacific
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, reaffirming both nations' commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• Discussions focused on enhancing defense cooperation, maritime security, and economic partnerships, particularly in infrastructure development and supply chain resilience.
• Both sides emphasized the importance of multilateral cooperation and adherence to international law in addressing regional challenges.
• Joint exercises and technology transfer in critical and emerging technologies were identified as key areas for future collaboration.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence of strategic interests between India and Japan amidst evolving geopolitical dynamics.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: Event: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue.
Key focus: Free and Open Indo-Pacific, defense, maritime security, economic partnership.
Areas of collaboration: Joint exercises, technology transfer.
Context: Evolving geopolitical dynamics in the Indo-Pacific region.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-Japan Special Strategic and Global Partnership in maintaining regional stability in the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss how India's Act East Policy aligns with Japan's vision for a free and open Indo-Pacific.
Evaluate the role of bilateral defense and security cooperation between India and Japan in countering maritime challenges and ensuring freedom of navigation.
Examine the potential for India-Japan collaboration in critical technologies and infrastructure development in third countries.
GS2.imageDescription: Foreign Ministers of India and Japan shaking hands during the strategic dialogue.
GS2.trivia: Japan is a significant investor in India's infrastructure, including the Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail project (Bullet Train).

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Right to Digital Privacy, Sets Guidelines for Data Protection
GS2.explainer:
• In a landmark judgment, the Supreme Court of India reaffirmed the fundamental 'Right to Digital Privacy' as an intrinsic part of Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty).
• The ruling came in a case challenging the indiscriminate collection and use of personal data by private entities and government agencies.
• The Court laid down stringent guidelines for data collection, processing, and storage, emphasizing consent, purpose limitation, and data minimization.
• It directed the government to expedite the enactment of a comprehensive data protection law, consistent with the principles outlined in the judgment.
• This decision is expected to have far-reaching implications for technology companies, data governance, and individual rights in the digital age.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary Ministries and Departments of the Government; pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity.
prelimsPoints: Court: Supreme Court of India.
Verdict: Right to Digital Privacy upheld as part of Article 21.
Key principles: Consent, purpose limitation, data minimization.
Directive: Expedite comprehensive data protection law.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's judgment on the Right to Digital Privacy for individual liberties and state surveillance in India.
Discuss the challenges in balancing national security concerns with the fundamental right to privacy in the digital realm.
Evaluate the adequacy of existing legal frameworks in India to protect personal data and suggest necessary reforms.
Examine the global best practices in data protection and how India can align its policies to meet international standards.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel resting on law books with a digital padlock icon superimposed, symbolizing digital justice.
GS2.trivia: The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) first declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

GS2.headline: India-Nepal Joint Working Group Concludes Talks on Border Management and Security
GS2.explainer:
• The 18th meeting of the India-Nepal Joint Working Group (JWG) on Border Management and Security concluded in Kathmandu.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to maintaining peace and tranquillity along the open border and addressing cross-border crimes.
• Discussions included enhancing cooperation on intelligence sharing, curbing human trafficking, drug smuggling, and illegal arms trade.
• The JWG reviewed progress on border infrastructure development and agreed on measures to streamline movement of people and goods.
• This dialogue is crucial for strengthening bilateral ties and ensuring mutual security along the 1,800-km long shared border.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
prelimsPoints: Event: India-Nepal Joint Working Group (JWG) meeting.
Focus: Border management, security, cross-border crime.
Location: Kathmandu.
Significance: Maintaining peace along the open border.
mainsPoints: Discuss the unique nature of the India-Nepal open border and the challenges it poses for border management and security.
Analyze the various forms of cross-border crimes prevalent along the India-Nepal border and their implications for internal security.
Evaluate the effectiveness of bilateral mechanisms like the Joint Working Group in addressing security concerns and fostering cooperation.
Examine the role of local communities in border management and the importance of socio-economic development in border areas.
GS2.imageDescription: A map highlighting the India-Nepal border, with symbols representing cooperation and security.
GS2.trivia: The India-Nepal border is unique for being an open border, allowing free movement of people, a provision that dates back to the 1950 Treaty of Peace and Friendship.

GS2.headline: National Health Mission Expands Telemedicine Services to Remote Areas
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare announced a significant expansion of telemedicine services under the National Health Mission (NHM).
• The initiative aims to enhance healthcare accessibility in remote, rural, and underserved regions of the country.
• The expansion includes upgrading digital infrastructure, training healthcare workers, and integrating AI-powered diagnostic tools.
• Telemedicine services will cover a wider range of specialties, including mental health, chronic disease management, and specialist consultations.
• This move is expected to bridge the gap in healthcare delivery, reduce patient travel, and improve health outcomes, particularly for vulnerable populations.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Scheme: National Health Mission (NHM).
Initiative: Expansion of telemedicine services.
Target areas: Remote, rural, underserved regions.
Key features: Digital infrastructure, training, AI diagnostics, wider specialties.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of telemedicine in transforming healthcare delivery in a vast and diverse country like India.
Analyze the challenges in implementing and scaling up telemedicine services, especially in rural and remote areas.
Evaluate the role of digital infrastructure and human resource development in ensuring equitable access to telemedicine.
Examine how telemedicine can contribute to achieving the goals of universal health coverage and the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) related to health.
GS2.imageDescription: A doctor interacting with a patient via a video call on a tablet, symbolizing telemedicine.
GS2.trivia: eSanjeevani, the national telemedicine service, has served millions of patients, becoming the world's largest government-owned telemedicine platform.

GS2.headline: India Advocates for Global South Interests at G20 Sherpa Meeting
GS2.explainer:
• India's G20 Sherpa actively championed the concerns and priorities of the Global South at the recent G20 Sherpa meeting.
• Key issues highlighted included equitable access to finance for sustainable development, climate justice, and food and energy security.
• India emphasized the need for reforms in multilateral development banks to better serve the needs of developing countries.
• The Sherpa meeting aimed to finalize the agenda and draft declarations for the upcoming G20 Leaders' Summit.
• India continues to play a pivotal role in bridging the gap between developed and developing nations within the G20 framework.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: Forum: G20 Sherpa meeting.
India's role: Advocating for Global South interests.
Key issues: Finance access, climate justice, food/energy security.
Focus: Reforms in multilateral development banks.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's growing leadership role in representing the interests of the Global South within international forums like the G20.
Analyze the challenges faced by developing countries in achieving sustainable development and climate justice in the current global economic order.
Evaluate the need for reforms in multilateral development banks to enhance their responsiveness to the needs of the Global South.
Examine how India's foreign policy prioritizes multilateralism and cooperation to address global challenges.
GS2.imageDescription: A collage of flags from G20 member countries with a focus on India's flag, symbolizing international diplomacy.
GS2.trivia: The G20 comprises 19 countries and the European Union, representing around 85% of global GDP, over 75% of global trade, and about two-thirds of the world population.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Reforms for Judicial Appointments and Accountability
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Law and Justice submitted a report recommending significant reforms in the system of judicial appointments and accountability.
• The report advocated for greater transparency in the Collegium system and suggested a more inclusive selection process involving greater public scrutiny.
• It proposed a clearer mechanism for addressing complaints against judges and enhancing judicial ethics.
• The committee also highlighted the need to fill judicial vacancies expeditiously to reduce pendency of cases.
• These recommendations are aimed at strengthening the independence of the judiciary while ensuring its accountability to the public.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary Ministries and Departments of the Government; pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity.
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies.
prelimsPoints: Body: Parliamentary Standing Committee on Law and Justice.
Recommendations: Reforms in judicial appointments, greater transparency in Collegium system.
Focus: Accountability, judicial ethics, filling vacancies.
Objective: Strengthen judicial independence and accountability.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the existing Collegium system for judicial appointments in India and the arguments for and against its reform.
Discuss the importance of judicial independence and accountability in a democratic setup and the challenges in balancing them.
Analyze the recommendations of the Parliamentary Committee for improving the transparency and efficiency of judicial appointments.
Examine the impact of judicial vacancies and case pendency on the delivery of justice in India and suggest comprehensive solutions.
GS2.imageDescription: A depiction of the Indian Parliament building and the Supreme Court building, symbolizing the interaction between legislative and judicial bodies.
GS2.trivia: The Collegium system, which evolved through Supreme Court judgments, currently governs the appointment and transfer of judges to the higher judiciary.

GS2.headline: Government Launches Nationwide Awareness Campaign on Mental Health and Well-being
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, in collaboration with various NGOs, launched a nationwide awareness campaign on mental health and well-being.
• The campaign aims to destigmatize mental illness, promote early intervention, and educate the public about available support systems.
• It targets diverse demographics, including youth, working professionals, and rural communities, through multi-platform outreach.
• Special focus is given to integrating mental health services at the primary healthcare level and training frontline workers.
• This initiative underscores the government's commitment to addressing the growing burden of mental health issues and promoting holistic well-being.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: Campaign: Nationwide awareness campaign on mental health.
Launched by: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (with NGOs).
Objectives: Destigmatize mental illness, promote early intervention, educate public.
Focus: Primary healthcare integration, frontline worker training.
mainsPoints: Discuss the increasing prevalence of mental health issues in India and the socio-economic factors contributing to this trend.
Analyze the challenges in addressing mental health concerns in India, particularly the issues of stigma, lack of awareness, and inadequate infrastructure.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives and policies in promoting mental health and well-being across different sections of society.
Suggest comprehensive strategies for integrating mental health services into primary healthcare and building a supportive ecosystem for individuals with mental illness.
GS2.imageDescription: A diverse group of people engaging in positive activities, with a thought bubble symbolizing mental well-being.
GS2.trivia: According to the World Health Organization, India accounts for nearly 15% of the global mental health burden.

GS2.headline: India and Sri Lanka Hold High-Level Dialogue on Maritime Security Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka convened a high-level dialogue in Colombo to enhance cooperation on maritime security in the Indian Ocean region.
• Discussions focused on joint surveillance, anti-piracy operations, combating illegal fishing, and intelligence sharing.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to ensuring a safe and secure maritime environment, crucial for regional trade and stability.
• The dialogue also addressed challenges posed by transnational crimes and the need for coordinated responses.
• This engagement underscores the strategic importance of bilateral maritime relations for both countries.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
keyTerms: SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region)
prelimsPoints: Event: High-level dialogue on maritime security.
Participants: India and Sri Lanka.
Location: Colombo.
Focus: Joint surveillance, anti-piracy, illegal fishing, intelligence sharing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the Indian Ocean Region for India's security and economic interests.
Analyze the common maritime security challenges faced by India and Sri Lanka and the need for bilateral cooperation.
Evaluate the effectiveness of joint mechanisms in combating transnational crimes and ensuring maritime safety.
Examine how India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy and SAGAR vision contribute to strengthening maritime partnerships in the region.
GS2.imageDescription: Naval ships from India and Sri Lanka conducting a joint exercise in the Indian Ocean.
GS2.trivia: India and Sri Lanka conduct several joint naval exercises, including 'SLINEX' and 'Dosti' (trilateral with Maldives), to enhance maritime cooperation.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Focuses on Taming Inflation and Sustaining Growth
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to maintain the repo rate at its current level for the sixth consecutive meeting.
• The decision comes amidst persistent global inflationary pressures and a focus on ensuring durable disinflation while supporting economic growth.
• RBI Governor highlighted the need for vigilance against rising food and energy prices, which could impact headline inflation.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to calibrating monetary policy to achieve the medium-term inflation target of 4% within a band of +/- 2%.
• Experts suggest that while growth remains robust, global uncertainties and domestic supply-side issues warrant a cautious approach.
